cash_app_customer_insights_segments.description: Customer Insights segmentation developed by the Customer Insights team based on money attitudes, usage behaviors, and socioeconomic data of Cash App users. This is an attitudinal segmentation that provides deep understanding of customer motivations and financial behaviors.

values.Access: Lowest income segment, constantly worried about finances. Looking for basic financial access. Represents customers who need better access to the financial system.
values.Simplicity: Looking to simplify money management, avoid complexity and fees. Values straightforward financial tools.
values.Growth: Seeking financial freedom and willing to take risks for reward. Growth-oriented mindset.
values.Security: Prioritizing safety and protection of their money. Risk-averse customers.
values.Certainty: Seeking predictability and stability in their financial lives.
values.Wealth: Highest income segment (~3X higher than Access). Financially sophisticated with more income, investments, and financial products used.

uses: Product feature prioritization based on segment needs
important_notes: Core Customers = Access + Simplicity + Growth segments (represent 50% of customer base)
Core customers use Cash App for more than just P2P and embody the mission
ML solution classifies 63M quarterly active users with tenure ≥3 months
Segmentation uses Artificial Neural Network with Keras (three hierarchical models)
Sample size N=14K is included in Experian data pulls for enhanced financial insights
cash_app_customer_insights_segments.data_freshness: Predictions as of 9/1/2023 for ~63M customer tokens

cash_app_age_generation_segments.description: Age and generational segmentation based on birth year obtained from Identity Verification (IDV). Critical for understanding life stage needs and product fit. Note: ~25% of customers haven't IDV'd so age is unknown.

values.Teen: Under 18 years old. Special account type with restrictions.
values.Gen Z: Born 1995-2015, currently under 29 years old. Digital natives, mobile-first users.
values.Millennial / Gen Y: Born 1980-1994, currently 29-45 years old. Largest segment, 1 in 2 customers are Millennial or Gen Z.
values.Gen X: Born 1965-1979, currently 45-60 years old. Established careers and families.
values.Baby Boomer: Born 1944-1964, currently 60+ years old. Approaching or in retirement.
values.Not IDVed: Customers who haven't completed identity verification. Age unknown, represents ~25% of customer base.

uses: Life stage targeting for product features (e.g., savings, investing, banking)
Understanding generational financial behaviors and preferences
cash_app_age_generation_segments.common_sql_pattern: CASE WHEN (2025 - birth_year) < 29 THEN '1995-2015 Gen Z' WHEN (2025 - birth_year) BETWEEN 29 AND 45 THEN '1980-1994 Gen Y Millennial' WHEN (2025 - birth_year) BETWEEN 45 AND 60 THEN '1965-1979 Gen X' WHEN (2025 - birth_year) >= 60 THEN '1944-1964 Baby Boomer' END AS Generation
important_notes: Birth year only available after successful IDV completion
Teen accounts have special restrictions and compliance requirements
Update year in calculation (currently 2025) for accurate age calculations
Consider filtering out 'Not IDVed' for age-specific analysis

cash_app_geographic_segments.description: Geographic segmentation based on customer location from IDV and geolocation signals. Critical for regional strategy, network effects analysis, and localized product features.
cash_app_geographic_segments.fields: LOC_COUNTRY, LOC_REGION, LOC_DIVISION, LOC_STATE, LOC_COUNTY, LOC_DMA, BEST_HOME_CITY, BEST_HOME_STATE, BEST_HOME_POSTAL_CODE

values.South: Nearly 2/3 of Cash App customers are in the Southern U.S. Highest long-term retention rate.
values.West: ~16% of new establishments (up from 14% in 2019). Lowest retention rate with sharpest negative slope.
values.Midwest: Similar retention to Northeast. Moderate customer concentration.
values.Northeast: Similar retention to Midwest. Moderate customer concentration.

important_notes: South region has highest concentration (~66%) and best retention
West region shows declining cohort health and lowest retention
Location data combines IDV information with geolocation signals
DMA (Designated Market Area) useful for advertising campaigns
Zip code-level data available for granular analysis

cash_app_income_segments.description: Income segmentation based on zip code household income data, adjusted using survey and census data. Designed to align with priority of moving up-market through banking products.

values.0-30k: Lowest income segment. Often overlaps with Access customer segment.
values.30k-70k: Core income segment. Primary target for banking products.
values.70k+: Higher income segment. Target for wealth-building products like investing and savings.
values.Core Income: Combination of 30k-70k segment, represents primary banking product opportunity.

uses: Product targeting for banking, investing, and savings features
Understanding financial capacity and product affordability
Pricing strategy and fee structure optimization
important_notes: Income is zip code-based, not individual customer income
Adjusted using survey and census data for accuracy
Wealth segment has ~3X higher income than Access segment
Income segmentation aligns with banking product adoption strategy
Consider combining with network_segment for 'Core' vs 'Non-Core' classification
cash_app_income_segments.common_sql_pattern: CASE WHEN income_segment IN ('3.30k-70k','4.0-30k') OR network_segment = 'Self-sustain' THEN 'Core' ELSE 'Non Core' END AS Core_Grouping

cash_app_network_segments.description: Geography-based segmentation that captures the virality and network effects of specific zip codes. Measures the health and strength of Cash App's social network in different areas. Network effects are fundamental to Cash App's competitive advantage.

values.Self-sustain: Strongest networks with highest virality. High in-group P2P activity. Self-sustaining growth through network effects. Best retention and engagement.
values.Invest: Growing networks requiring investment. Moderate network health. Opportunity for growth with proper investment.
values.Reassess: Weakest networks with lowest virality. May require strategic reassessment or different approach.

network_segment_scores.1: Strongest network health. Highest engagement and virality.
network_segment_scores.3: Moderate network health. Room for improvement.

uses: Cohort retention analysis by network strength
important_notes: Network effects differentiate Cash App from competitors like Venmo
People use Cash App BECAUSE their social network uses Cash App
Subnetworks are groupings of zip codes based on high in-group P2P activity
Self-sustain segment often combined with income segments for 'Core' classification
Network strength correlates with retention and long-term value
More new establishments occurring in higher penetration areas
cash_app_network_segments.common_sql_pattern: CASE WHEN network_segment = 'Self-sustain' OR income_segment IN ('30k-70k') THEN 'Core' ELSE 'Non Core' END
cash_app_network_segments.competitive_advantage: Cash App's network is fundamentally different from competitors. People engage more on Cash App than Venmo, with more local use cases (buying goods/services in-person) and participatory activities (lending money, sharing expenses).

cash_app_account_type_segments.description: Account type segmentation distinguishing between personal, business, family, and teen accounts. Critical for product features, compliance, and user experience customization.

values.Personal: Standard individual consumer accounts. Majority of customer base.
values.Business: Business accounts for merchants and service providers. Different fee structures and features.
values.Teen: Accounts for users under 18. Special restrictions and parental controls. Growing segment.
values.Families - Sponsor: Parent/guardian account sponsoring dependent accounts. Access to family features.
values.Families - Dependent: Teen or dependent account linked to sponsor. Limited features and spending controls.

uses: Compliance and regulatory requirements (especially for teens)
important_notes: Teen accounts have specific compliance requirements
Business accounts may have different rate plans and fees
Families program enables sponsored dependent accounts
Some accounts were 'forced business' due to transaction patterns
Account type affects available features and product access

cash_app_lifecycle_cohorts.description: Customer lifecycle segmentation based on key milestone dates including account creation, first money movement, and establishment. Critical for cohort analysis and retention measurement.
cash_app_lifecycle_cohorts.fields: NEW_CUSTOMER_AT, FIRST_SUCCESSFUL_MOVEMENT_DATE, MOVEMENT_COHORT_MONTH, MOVEMENT_COHORT_YEAR, ESTABLISHMENT_DATE, CUSTOMER_CREATED_AT

values.Pre-2020: Customers who joined before 2020. Most established user base. ~50% of customers.
values.2020 Cohort: Largest acquisition cohort ever. Pandemic-driven growth. Unique behavioral patterns.
values.2021 Cohort: Second largest acquisition cohort. Continued pandemic growth.
values.2022+ Cohorts: Post-pandemic cohorts. Different acquisition dynamics and retention patterns.

key_milestones.NEW_CUSTOMER_AT: When user became a Cash App customer (requires app install and linked alias)
key_milestones.FIRST_SUCCESSFUL_MOVEMENT_DATE: Date of first successful money movement (proxy for active date)
key_milestones.ESTABLISHMENT_DATE: When customer became 'established' (3+ successful money movements in 30-day window)
key_milestones.MOVEMENT_COHORT_MONTH/YEAR: Cohort based on first money movement timing

uses: Understanding cohort quality differences over time
important_notes: 2020 and 2021 were largest acquisition cohorts in Cash App history
Cohort retention has been deteriorating, especially in recent cohorts
Establishment requires 3+ successful money movements in 30 days
Different cohorts show different product adoption patterns
Cohort degradation most pronounced in low penetration MSAs and West region

cash_app_acquisition_channels.description: Customer acquisition channel segmentation tracking how customers discovered and signed up for Cash App. Critical for marketing attribution and CAC optimization.

values.Organic: Largest channel. Most sign-ups are organic. Note: Due to privacy changes on mobile, 'organic' likely contains consumers driven by paid marketing.
values.Invitations: Second largest channel. Users invited by existing customers.
values.Referrals: Third largest channel. Formal referral program participants.
values.Paid Marketing: Paid acquisition channels including social, search, display.
values.Partner Channels: Partnerships and integrations with other platforms.

first_platform_values.iOS: Apple iPhone/iPad users. Typically higher income demographic.
first_platform_values.Android: Android device users. Broader demographic reach.

uses: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis by channel
Understanding organic vs paid growth dynamics
important_notes: Organic is largest channel but may include unattributed paid marketing
Privacy changes (iOS 14+) have impacted deterministic attribution
Invitations and referrals leverage network effects
Cohort degradation exists across all channels but most pronounced in Organic
Subchannel provides more granular attribution including partner channels

cash_app_product_usage_segments.description: Segmentation based on product adoption and usage patterns. Measures customer engagement across Cash App's product suite including P2P, Banking, Investing, and other features.

values.P2P Only: Customers only use Cash App for peer-to-peer payments. Single product users.
values.Banking Users: Using Cash Card, Direct Deposit, or other banking features. Fastest growing segment.
values.Multi-Product Users: Using 2+ product lines. Higher engagement and retention.
values.Primary Banking Actives: Customers using Cash App as their primary bank. Highest value segment.
values.Bitcoin Users: Active with Bitcoin buying/selling. Includes Sat Stackers and Crypto Traders.
values.Cash App Pay Users: Using Cash App Pay for merchant payments.

habituation_segments.Daily Actives: 1 in 10 customers use Cash App every day. Highest engagement.
habituation_segments.Weekly Actives: Just under 50% use Cash App at least weekly. Core engaged users.
habituation_segments.Monthly Actives (MAU+): Active on monthly basis. Customers active weekly or daily have distinctly different value.
habituation_segments.Occasional Users: Less than monthly usage. Lower engagement.

product_specific_fields.MONTHS_ACTIVE_IN_YEAR_[PRODUCT]: Count of months active with each product

important_notes: 50% of customers still P2P-only, but multi-product usage is growing
Banking suite (Cash Card, Direct Deposit) leads multi-product growth
Weekly+ actives produce distinctly different LTV than less frequent users
BTC & Equities early attachers show weaker cohort retention than P2P/Banking
Primary Banking Actives represent highest value customer segment
Habituation status critical for retention and long-term value

cash_app_financial_behavior_segments.description: Segmentation based on financial behaviors including linked payment methods, money movement patterns, and banking engagement. Critical for understanding customer financial health and product fit.

values.Bank Account Linked: Has active bank account linked. Indicates banking relationship and trust.
values.Debit Card Linked: Has debit card linked. Most common payment method.
values.Credit Card Linked: Has credit card linked. May indicate different spending patterns.
values.Money Mover: Has successfully moved money through platform. Key activation milestone.
values.No Payment Method: No linked payment methods. Friction point for activation.

key_milestones.FIRST_BANK_ACCOUNT_LINKED_AT: When customer first linked bank account
key_milestones.FIRST_DEBIT_CARD_LINKED_AT: When customer first linked debit card
key_milestones.FIRST_CREDIT_CARD_LINKED_AT: When customer first linked credit card
key_milestones.FIRST_CARD_LINKED_AT: When customer first linked any card

uses: Product feature eligibility (e.g., Direct Deposit requires bank link)
important_notes: Bank account linking indicates higher trust and engagement
Money movement is critical activation milestone
Multiple payment methods linked indicates higher engagement
Payment method availability affects feature access
Timing of payment method linking affects retention

cash_app_demographic_ethnicity_age.description: Zip code-level demographic data including ethnicity and age distributions. Useful for understanding community demographics and targeting.

values.ZIP_PRIMARY_ETHNICITY: Majority ethnicity in customer's zip code based on census data
values.ZIP_MEDIAN_AGE: Median age in customer's zip code

important_notes: Data is zip code-level, not individual customer level
Based on census and demographic data sources
Useful for community-level insights
Hispanic/Latino opportunity identified as strategic focus
Consider combining with individual-level data when available

cash_app_risk_compliance_segments.description: Risk and compliance-based segmentation for fraud prevention, regulatory compliance, and customer protection. Critical for platform safety and regulatory adherence.
cash_app_risk_compliance_segments.fields: BLACKLISTED, DENYLIST_STATUS, IS_DENYLISTED, IS_CLOSED, SPECIAL_STATUS_LEVEL, SEGMENT

values.Not Denylisted: Good standing customers. No risk flags.
values.Denylisted: Customers blocked due to fraud, compliance, or risk issues.
values.Payment Blocks: ~35% of established cohorts. Affects both authentic and inauthentic customers. Largest drag on cohort quality.
values.Special Status: Customers requiring additional monitoring or review.

uses: Understanding impact of risk actions on retention
important_notes: Payment blocks cause most drag on cohort quality (~35% of cohorts)
Payment blocks affect both authentic and inauthentic customers
Perceptions of scams outweigh actual scams on Cash App
Trust issues can decrease non-customer adoption
Risk actions have significant impact on cohort retention
Filter out denylisted customers for clean analytics (use denylist_status = 'Not Denylisted')
